info.name: TMS Parking Validation
info.description:
## TMS Parking Validation API

### Token-Switching Login Flow

The TMS login is a **two-step token switch** — the client never holds an OAuth token directly.

**Server-side flow on `POST /api/auth/login`:**
1. Backend calls the OAuth server (password grant) with the supplied credentials.
2. Validates the OAuth JWT signature using the RSA public key (`tms-jwt-pub.jks`).
3. **Upserts** a `login` row — creates on first login, updates `last_login_date` / `gtoken` / `email` on subsequent logins.
4. Loads `role`, `tenantId`, `clientId`, `subTenantId` from the `users` table.
5. Signs a **new TMS-internal HMAC-SHA256 JWT** embedding the full tenant context.
6. Returns the internal JWT as `accessToken` — the raw OAuth token is **never** sent to the client.

### Quick Start
1. Set the `password` variable (and optionally `oauthBaseUrl` / `username`).
2. **Run Login** — `accessToken`, `refreshToken`, `userId`, `role`, `tenantId`, `clientId`, `subTenantId` are auto-saved.
3. All protected requests inherit `Bearer {{accessToken}}` from the collection auth.

variable.description: OAuth server base URL (server-side only — for reference)
Set to your OAuth RS256 token for MODE 1 login. Leave empty for MODE 2 (username/password).
TMS-internal HMAC-SHA256 JWT — set by Login
OAuth refresh token — set by Login

item.name: 🔐 Auth
item.description:
Authentication endpoints.

Implements the **OAuth2 password-grant → TMS internal token switching** flow.
The OAuth server (`{{oauthBaseUrl}}`) is called server-side — the client only receives a TMS-internal HMAC-SHA256 JWT.

exec: // MODE 1 (old-code flow): set collection variable 'oauthBearerToken' to your OAuth RS256 token
//         → request will send it as Authorization: Bearer header, no body needed
console.log('MODE 1: OAuth Bearer token login (old-code flow)');
// Claims: sub (userId), email, role, tenantId, clientId, subTenantId, iat, exp
console.log('=== Internal JWT payload ===', JSON.stringify(p, null, 2));
console.log('userId:', p.sub, '| role:', p.role, '| tenantId:', p.tenantId || '(none)');
console.error('Could not decode internal JWT:', e.message);

request.description:
## Login — Two Modes

Matches the old-code `OauthTokenFilter` + `TmsAuthenticationSuccessHandler` flow.

---

### MODE 1 — OAuth Bearer token *(old-code behaviour)*

You already have an OAuth RS256 token from the OAuth server. Pass it as `Authorization: Bearer`:

```
POST /api/auth/login
Authorization: Bearer <your_oauth_rs256_token>
(no body required)
```

**In Postman:** set the `oauthBearerToken` collection variable to your OAuth token. The pre-request script will inject it as the `Authorization` header automatically.

---

### MODE 2 — Username + password *(new convenience flow)*

Let TMS call the OAuth server on your behalf:

```json
POST /api/auth/login
{
  "username": "{{username}}",
  "password": "{{password}}"
}
```

**In Postman:** leave `oauthBearerToken` empty and set `username` / `password`.

---

### Both modes return the same response (`AuthTokens`):

```json
{
  "accessToken":  "<TMS-internal HMAC-SHA256 JWT>",
  "refreshToken": "<OAuth refresh token or null>",
  "expiresIn":    28800,
  "tokenType":    "Bearer"
}
```

**Use `accessToken` as `Authorization: Bearer` for all subsequent TMS API calls.**

The test script decodes the internal JWT and saves `userId`, `role`, `tenantId`, etc. as collection variables.

## Token Exchange — OAuth RS256 → TMS HS256

Use this if you **already have an OAuth RS256 access token** obtained directly from the OAuth server and want a TMS-internal JWT.

### When to use
- You called `{{oauthBaseUrl}}/oauth/token` yourself and got an RS256 token.
- You tried using the OAuth token as Bearer on a TMS endpoint and got:
  `Unsupported JWS algorithm RS256, must be HS256`

### Server-side flow
1. Validates the OAuth token signature (RSA public key).
2. Upserts the `login` row (first-login creates it; subsequent logins update `last_login_date` / `gtoken`).
3. Loads user role + tenant context from the `users` table.
4. Signs a new TMS-internal HMAC-SHA256 JWT with full tenant context.
5. Returns the internal JWT as `accessToken`.

### Response
```json
{
  "accessToken": "<TMS-internal HS256 JWT>",
  "refreshToken": null,
  "expiresIn":    28800,
  "tokenType":    "Bearer"
}
```

> No `refreshToken` is returned because only the OAuth access token was provided. Use `POST /api/auth/login` with username/password for full login with refresh.

exec: console.log('Refreshed | role:', p.role, '| exp:', new Date(p.exp * 1000).toISOString());
} catch (e) { console.error('Could not decode refreshed JWT:', e.message); }

request.description:
Rotates the OAuth refresh token and re-signs a fresh TMS-internal JWT.

Because the server re-loads the user from DB, any **role or tenant assignment changes** made by an admin since last login are picked up here.

**Refresh token rotation is enforced** — replaying an old refresh token returns `403` (reuse detection).

exec: console.log('Me:', res.email, '| userId:', res.userId, '| roles:', res.roles);

request.description:
Returns the authenticated user's profile decoded from the TMS-internal JWT.

**Response (`AuthUser`):**
```json
{
  "userId": "auth0|abc123",
  "email": "user@example.com",
  "name": "Jane Doe",
  "tenantId": "550e8400-...",
  "clientId": "6ba7b810-...",
  "subTenantId": null,
  "roles": ["TENANT_ADMIN"]
}
```

Revokes the OAuth refresh token and clears the Redis session.

Requires `Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}`.

Returns `204 No Content`.

Valid roles: `ADMIN`, `CLIENT_ADMIN`, `TENANT_ADMIN`, `SUBTENANT_USER`, `VIEWER`.

After a role change the user must refresh their token for the new role to be embedded in the next internal JWT.
